name: Character-Based LCD Display
description: The `lcd_gpio` version of this component addresses the screen directly and does not employ a GPIO expander module. Each of the data pins of the LCD needs a dedicated GPIO pin on the ESP. Connecting the screen this way offers faster refresh, especially in conjunction with an [LCD Menu](https://esphome.io/components/display_menu/lcd_menu#lcd_menu).

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to re-draw the screen. Defaults to `1s`.

config_entries.label: Data Pins
config_entries.description: A list of the data pins you have hooked up to the LCD. The list can either be 4 items long (operating in 4-bit mode with either the first 4 data pins connected or the last 4 data pins connected), or 8 items long (when you have connected all 8 data pins).

config_entries.label: Dimensions
config_entries.description: The dimensions of the display with `COLUMNSxROWS`. If you're not sure, power the display on, turn contrast high up and just count them.

config_entries.label: Enable Pin
config_entries.description: The pin you have `E` (`06`  ) hooked up to.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Rs Pin
config_entries.description: The pin you have `RS` (`04`  ) hooked up to.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Rw Pin
config_entries.description: Optionally set the pin you have `R/W` (`05`  ) hooked up to. You can also just permanently connect that pin to `GND`.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: The lambda to use for rendering the content on the display. See [Rendering Lambda](https://esphome.io/components/display/lcd_display#display-lcd_lambda) for more information.
